commits.author: Kimon N.
commits.message:
snap: set MOZ_APP_REMOTINGNAME to match snapd-mangled desktop filename (#63)

snapd installs the app's desktop entry as thunderbird_thunderbird.desktop.
Wayland compositors (notably KDE KWin) match windows to desktop entries
by comparing the xdg_toplevel app_id to the .desktop filename. Without
this env var, Thunderbird advertises its application.ini RemotingName
as the app_id (thunderbird-esr for ESR, thunderbird for release), which
doesn't match thunderbird_thunderbird.desktop -- KDE Plasma on Wayland
then falls back to a generic Wayland icon.

Same one-line fix as canonical/firefox-snap#74; already applied on the
beta branch in 42a6e83 but never forward-ported.

Fixes: #41

commits.author: Sergio Costas Rodriguez
commits.message:
Remove gnome-platform folder

Currently, the snap creates the `gnome-platform` folder as a
workaround for an old bug (LP #2016358).

This workaround isn't used anymore in Firefox, since 2a9002573535c8fb6158e46f601c3c541dea6a46.
Instead it is replaced by gpu-2404.

This PR does the same change.

that this PR also fixes.

Some notable things that `ubuntu_lints` enforces:
- `final` for variables that aren't reassigned
- Strict types where possible (this results in a fair amount of casting
due to the nature of the library, please double check these)
- Trailing commas

Also done for packagekit.dart:

commits.message:
fix: deserialization for PromptCase with interface inference (#306)

This fixes a deserialization issue for the scripted client's
`TypedPromptCase` enum.

The `untagged` Serde macro previously defaulted to the first loose match
based on definition order. With the new `deserialize_prompt_case
function`, we can now identify the correct interface based on JSON
fields. To avoid ambiguity, we should mandate an `interface` field in
all prompt-filter JSON objects.

Fixes #305

---

commits.author: Gabriele Belluardo
commits.message: fix: deserialization for PromptCase with interface inference

commits.author: Gabriele Belluardo
commits.message:
fix: remove blocking channel send from spawn_for_output (#304)

Previously, `spawn_for_output` would wait for the subprocess which
triggered a prompt to finish, then manually read the stdout and stderr
pipes before sending the output over a MPSC channel. This seemed to be
the cause of tests sometimes hanging after the subprocess exited.

In particular, with the prompting notice backend enabled in snapd, the
prompting-client integration tests consistently hang, e.g.
`happy_path_read_single`, which hangs after the successful POST of the
reply to the prompt. At that point, the triggering process ought to
finish and let the test succeed, but for some reason, the tests hang.
The PR to enable the prompting notice backend in snapd can be found at
https://github.com/canonical/snapd/pull/16303

This PR replaces the manual wait and reading of stdout/stderr with
instead calling `wait_with_output` and directly using the resulting
output stdout/stderr, without a separate reader. Then, importantly,
rather than sending the output over a MPSC, `spawn_for_output` now
returns a `JoinHandle` directly. This means that callers no longer have
a blocking `recv` call from a MPSC channel; instead, they await the
output of the subprocess thread handle, using a tokio timeout. This
prevents the test from hanging as a result of the tokio scheduler,
regardless of the order in which processes interact and how quickly
snapd reacts.

deps(go-server): bump github.com/jackc/pgx/v5 from 5.9.1 to 5.9.2 in /server in the minor-updates group (#339) Bumps the minor-updates group in /server with 1 update: [github.com/jackc/pgx/v5](https://github.com/jackc/pgx). Updates `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5` from 5.9.1 to 5.9.2
Changelog

Sourced from github.com/jackc/pgx/v5's changelog.

5.9.2 (April 18, 2026)

Fix SQL Injection via placeholder confusion with dollar quoted string literals (GHSA-j88v-2chj-qfwx)

SQL injection can occur when:

  1. The non-default simple protocol is used.
  2. A dollar quoted string literal is used in the SQL query.
  3. That query contains text that would be would be interpreted outside as a placeholder outside of a string literal.
  4. The value of that placeholder is controllable by the attacker.

e.g.

attackValue := `$tag$; drop table canary; --`
_, err = tx.Exec(ctx, `select $tag$ $1 $tag$, $1`,
pgx.QueryExecModeSimpleProtocol, attackValue)

This is unlikely to occur outside of a contrived scenario.

releases.title: Ubuntu22.04LTS/2204.1.22
releases.body:
Issues / bug reports

  • Addressed crashes on the slide show application reported by the store insights (#199) by better handling exit requests (https://github.com/ubuntu/ubuntu-wsl-splash/pull/25)
  • Enabled collecting debug symbols of all compiled binaries as release assets for future crash analysis (#263, #261, #260, https://github.com/ubuntu/ubuntu-wsl-splash/pull/27 and #1036)
  • Added more languages to the slide show (https://github.com/ubuntu/ubuntu-wsl-splash/pull/18, https://github.com/ubuntu/ubuntu-wsl-splash/pull/21, https://github.com/ubuntu/ubuntu-wsl-splash/pull/23 and https://github.com/ubuntu/ubuntu-wsl-splash/pull/24)

Other enhancements

  • Updated rootfs image to Ubuntu 22.04.1.
  • Implemented a patch for the release upgrader policy (#239 and #264)
  • Updated the store listing to point to the correct Ubuntu WSL website (#247)

What’s Changed

Other enhancements are mostly relevant to the Ubuntu Preview app, which is not directly related to this release.

  • Added check around optional splash access by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/207
  • Fix no-return lambda warning by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/209
  • Enable launcher command after exiting by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/210
  • QA is cancelled when a new commit is pushed by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/215
  • Bump runner version for the Jira sync workflow by @GabrielNagy in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/216
  • This app rootdir by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/220
  • Prevents running the build workflow on draft PRs by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/219
  • Enables checking if localhost forwarding is disabled by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/218
  • Created class to deal with interprocess syncronization by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/221
  • Solved some clang-tidy complaints by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/224
  • Better testing proposal by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/222
  • Refresh releases and assets by @github-actions in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/227
  • Enables MSBuild to update and build the Flutter app if required by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/229
  • Created class to temporarily set root as Ubuntu WSL user safely by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/223
  • Extracts the process handling out of the splash controller by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/233
  • Enables downloading images from the c-i.u.c/wsl by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/235
  • Skip C++ source linting for unrelevant PRs by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/236
  • Refresh releases and assets by @github-actions in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/230
  • Prevents filesystem exceptions by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/237
  • Adapts the image base name to the new pipeline by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/238
  • Removed Sudo::WslLaunch by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/240
  • showConsoleWindow accepts topWindow nullptr by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/243
  • [CI] Sets a 20 min timeout for clang-tidy by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/245
  • [CI] Attempts to avoid break when diff consists of one whitespace only by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/244
  • Abstraction on top of Win32 Named Events that can be set only once by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/242
  • Temporarily fix flutter-action version by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/246
  • Refresh releases and assets by @github-actions in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/248
  • Selection of default upgrade policy by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/239
  • Updates link ubuntu.com/wsl for store listing by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/247
  • Use strings.Fields to split the checksum file. by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/241
  • Checks whether the rootfs has UDI or Subiquity snaps by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/249
  • Models the environment variable LAUNCHER_FORCE_MODE by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/251
  • Refactor Application Strategies by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/250
  • Updates ubuntu-wsl-splash by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/252
  • Let SetOnceNamedEvent wait for confirmation by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/253
  • New app strategy for ARM64 in the context of the new OOBE by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/254
  • Implements the WinOobeStrategy by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/255
  • No need for the registration event in reconfiguration workflow by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/257
  • Traverse the filesystem only once when looking for UDI and Subiquity snaps. by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/258
  • Per-app #defines by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/256
  • Refactored starts_with and ends_with by @EduardGomezEscandell in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/259
  • Upload program debug databases by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/260
  • Updates splash submodule post PDB changes by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/261
  • WindowTitle is the one which may have LTS in by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/264
  • Implements the release workflow by @CarlosNihelton in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/263

New Contributors

  • @EduardGomezEscandell made their first contribution in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/207
  • @GabrielNagy made their first contribution in https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/pull/216

Full Changelog: https://github.com/ubuntu/WSL/compare/Ubuntu22.04LTS/2204.0.10…Ubuntu22.04LTS/2204.1.22

Issues / bug reports

  • Handle misreported error during installation when the GUI exits with SIGSEV due an OpenGL implementation issue (Installer reported failure but user was created · Issue #180 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Eliminate a race condition causing the installer window to never close under certain conditions on latest Windows builds ([Ubuntu-Preview] Installer GUI won’t close · Issue #201 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Prevent the UI to translate itself into languages that require extra fonts not shipped with our rootfs (Missing fonts in the OOBE · Issue #172 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Disallow selecting system-reserved usernames (OOBE - Disallow creation of system users and existing users · Issue #179 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Hide the console window only if the installation was initiated from start menu or from the Windows store (Windows terminal hidden on first run of ubuntu2204.exe · Issue #176 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Disable the hyperlink in the profile page due inability to invoke a web browser inside WSL at installation (The link https://aka.ms/wslusers does nothing · Issue #173 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Make the slide show “We are almost done” dialog non-dismissible (User can dismiss “We are almost done” dialog · Issue #177 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Fix the incomplete hint about the /etc/fstab file (WSL fstab hint by CarlosNihelton · Pull Request #855 · canonical/ubuntu-desktop-installer )
  • Avoid crash when user hits the HELP button on TUI (Installer crashes when users selects “Help” · Issue #202 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Create versionless icons for sizes smaller than 32px (Create square versionless icons if size < 32px · Issue #193 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Prevent printing error messages related to username and language acquisition to prefill the UI (Recovery user information failed with code 80070534 · Issue #170 · ubuntu/WSL )
  • Started addressing the long time applying changes by avoiding repeated work during that phase (Installing language packs takes a while · Issue #187 · ubuntu/WSL )

Other enhancements

  • Update theme to latest Yaru (Update yaru & dbus + dependencies by jpnurmi · Pull Request #868 · canonical/ubuntu-desktop-installer ). Wizard now transitions horizontally, alla iOS style.

deps(ci): bump actions/download-artifact from 7 to 8 in the gh-actions group (#45) Bumps the gh-actions group with 1 update: [actions/download-artifact](https://github.com/actions/download-artifact). Updates `actions/download-artifact` from 7 to 8
Release notes

Sourced from actions/download-artifact's releases.

v8.0.0

v8 - What's new

Direct downloads

To support direct uploads in actions/upload-artifact, the action will no longer attempt to unzip all downloaded files. Instead, the action checks the Content-Type header ahead of unzipping and skips non-zipped files. Callers wishing to download a zipped file as-is can also set the new skip-decompress parameter to false.

Enforced checks (breaking)

A previous release introduced digest checks on the download. If a download hash didn't match the expected hash from the server, the action would log a warning. Callers can now configure the behavior on mismatch with the digest-mismatch parameter. To be secure by default, we are now defaulting the behavior to error which will fail the workflow run.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Don't attempt to un-zip non-zipped downloads by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#460
  • Add a setting to specify what to do on hash mismatch and default it to error by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#461

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/download-artifact/compare/v7...v8.0.0

commits.author: Carlos Nihelton
commits.message:
fix(CI): Shutdown the WSL VM before validating the instance state in integration tests (#48)

It looks like a shutdown of WSL before validating the image is
beneficial in CI, we had three successful runs in a row after replacing
`wsl --terminate instance` with `wsl --shutdown`, see
[1](https://github.com/ubuntu/wsl-setup/actions/runs/23439953757),
[2](https://github.com/ubuntu/wsl-setup/actions/runs/23440423370) and
[3](https://github.com/ubuntu/wsl-setup/actions/runs/23441471549).

Some historical facts to get started:

- Last in-sequence successful integration test happened on Jan 20th,
2026
- WSL was upgraded in GH runners on Jan 9th, we had at least one
successful in-sequence run with that version of WSL. Since then no
changes to WSL in GH hosted runners.
- The version of cloud-init on the last successful and the first failed
runs were the same: v25.3.
- Recent images of resolute come with cloud-init v26.1
- The last CI failure was already running with that image.

So it doesn’t seem a particular issue with a version of cloud-init or
WSL.

The symptom is always the same (not easily reproducible locally):

```
 UNIT              LOAD   ACTIVE SUB    DESCRIPTION
● user@1001.service loaded failed failed User Manager for UID 1001

Legend: LOAD   → Reflects whether the unit definition was properly loaded.
        ACTIVE → The high-level unit activation state, i.e. generalization of SUB.
        SUB    → The low-level unit activation state, values depend on unit type.

1 loaded units listed.
```

My small historical analysis since a little before the failure trend
started until now didn't reveal any culprits or even suspects, but I
realized that it would be nice to have kept the image manifests over
time to compare the packages and versions. This doesn't seem the
particular case where having that would help, but future problem solving
might benefit from this idea, so I'm modifying the `get-wsl-image`
action to download the image manifest and upload it as a CI artefact.

---

UDENG-9298

commits.message:
fix: deserialization for PromptCase with interface inference (#306)

This fixes a deserialization issue for the scripted client's
`TypedPromptCase` enum.

The `untagged` Serde macro previously defaulted to the first loose match
based on definition order. With the new `deserialize_prompt_case
function`, we can now identify the correct interface based on JSON
fields. To avoid ambiguity, we should mandate an `interface` field in
all prompt-filter JSON objects.

Fixes #305

---

UDENG-9866

commits.author: Gabriele Belluardo
commits.message: fix: deserialization for PromptCase with interface inference

commits.author: Gabriele Belluardo
commits.message:
fix: remove blocking channel send from spawn_for_output (#304)

Previously, `spawn_for_output` would wait for the subprocess which
triggered a prompt to finish, then manually read the stdout and stderr
pipes before sending the output over a MPSC channel. This seemed to be
the cause of tests sometimes hanging after the subprocess exited.

In particular, with the prompting notice backend enabled in snapd, the
prompting-client integration tests consistently hang, e.g.
`happy_path_read_single`, which hangs after the successful POST of the
reply to the prompt. At that point, the triggering process ought to
finish and let the test succeed, but for some reason, the tests hang.
The PR to enable the prompting notice backend in snapd can be found at
https://github.com/canonical/snapd/pull/16303

This PR replaces the manual wait and reading of stdout/stderr with
instead calling `wait_with_output` and directly using the resulting
output stdout/stderr, without a separate reader. Then, importantly,
rather than sending the output over a MPSC, `spawn_for_output` now
returns a `JoinHandle` directly. This means that callers no longer have
a blocking `recv` call from a MPSC channel; instead, they await the
output of the subprocess thread handle, using a tokio timeout. This
prevents the test from hanging as a result of the tokio scheduler,
regardless of the order in which processes interact and how quickly
snapd reacts.

commits.author: Adrian Dombeck
commits.message:
ci: Automatically cancel in-progress workflow runs on push

If commits are pushed to the same PR in quick succession, the CI jobs
run multiple times in parallel, even though older runs are usually not
needed. This change saves some resources by automatically cancelling
in-progress workflows on new pushes to the same branch.

The implementation is directly from the example of the concurrency keyword:
https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-syntax#example-using-concurrency-and-the-default-behavior

commits.message:
deps(ci): bump actions/download-artifact from 7 to 8 in the gh-actions group (#45) Bumps the gh-actions group with 1 update: [actions/download-artifact](https://github.com/actions/download-artifact). Updates `actions/download-artifact` from 7 to 8
Release notes

Sourced from actions/download-artifact's releases.

v8.0.0

v8 - What's new

Direct downloads

To support direct uploads in actions/upload-artifact, the action will no longer attempt to unzip all downloaded files. Instead, the action checks the Content-Type header ahead of unzipping and skips non-zipped files. Callers wishing to download a zipped file as-is can also set the new skip-decompress parameter to false.

Enforced checks (breaking)

A previous release introduced digest checks on the download. If a download hash didn't match the expected hash from the server, the action would log a warning. Callers can now configure the behavior on mismatch with the digest-mismatch parameter. To be secure by default, we are now defaulting the behavior to error which will fail the workflow run.

ESM

To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

What's Changed

  • Don't attempt to un-zip non-zipped downloads by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#460
  • Add a setting to specify what to do on hash mismatch and default it to error by @​danwkennedy in actions/download-artifact#461

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/download-artifact/compare/v7...v8.0.0

commits.author: Carlos Nihelton
commits.message:
fix(CI): Shutdown the WSL VM before validating the instance state in integration tests (#48)

It looks like a shutdown of WSL before validating the image is
beneficial in CI, we had three successful runs in a row after replacing
`wsl --terminate instance` with `wsl --shutdown`, see
[1](https://github.com/ubuntu/wsl-setup/actions/runs/23439953757),
[2](https://github.com/ubuntu/wsl-setup/actions/runs/23440423370) and
[3](https://github.com/ubuntu/wsl-setup/actions/runs/23441471549).

Some historical facts to get started:

- Last in-sequence successful integration test happened on Jan 20th,
2026
- WSL was upgraded in GH runners on Jan 9th, we had at least one
successful in-sequence run with that version of WSL. Since then no
changes to WSL in GH hosted runners.
- The version of cloud-init on the last successful and the first failed
runs were the same: v25.3.
- Recent images of resolute come with cloud-init v26.1
- The last CI failure was already running with that image.

So it doesn’t seem a particular issue with a version of cloud-init or
WSL.

The symptom is always the same (not easily reproducible locally):

```
 UNIT              LOAD   ACTIVE SUB    DESCRIPTION
● user@1001.service loaded failed failed User Manager for UID 1001

Legend: LOAD   → Reflects whether the unit definition was properly loaded.
        ACTIVE → The high-level unit activation state, i.e. generalization of SUB.
        SUB    → The low-level unit activation state, values depend on unit type.

1 loaded units listed.
```

My small historical analysis since a little before the failure trend
started until now didn't reveal any culprits or even suspects, but I
realized that it would be nice to have kept the image manifests over
time to compare the packages and versions. This doesn't seem the
particular case where having that would help, but future problem solving
might benefit from this idea, so I'm modifying the `get-wsl-image`
action to download the image manifest and upload it as a CI artefact.

---

UDENG-9298

Adds `ubuntu_lints` to the project, which results in many syntax errors
that this PR also fixes.

Some notable things that `ubuntu_lints` enforces:
- `final` for variables that aren't reassigned
- Strict types where possible (this results in a fair amount of casting
due to the nature of the library, please double check these)
- Trailing commas

Also done for packagekit.dart:

commits.author: Gabriele Belluardo
commits.message:
fix: deserialization for PromptCase with interface inference (#306)

This fixes a deserialization issue for the scripted client's
`TypedPromptCase` enum.

The `untagged` Serde macro previously defaulted to the first loose match
based on definition order. With the new `deserialize_prompt_case
function`, we can now identify the correct interface based on JSON
fields. To avoid ambiguity, we should mandate an `interface` field in
all prompt-filter JSON objects.

Fixes #305

---

UDENG-9866

commits.author: Gabriele Belluardo
commits.message: fix: deserialization for PromptCase with interface inference

commits.author: Gabriele Belluardo
commits.message:
fix: remove blocking channel send from spawn_for_output (#304)

Previously, `spawn_for_output` would wait for the subprocess which
triggered a prompt to finish, then manually read the stdout and stderr
pipes before sending the output over a MPSC channel. This seemed to be
the cause of tests sometimes hanging after the subprocess exited.

In particular, with the prompting notice backend enabled in snapd, the
prompting-client integration tests consistently hang, e.g.
`happy_path_read_single`, which hangs after the successful POST of the
reply to the prompt. At that point, the triggering process ought to
finish and let the test succeed, but for some reason, the tests hang.
The PR to enable the prompting notice backend in snapd can be found at
https://github.com/canonical/snapd/pull/16303

This PR replaces the manual wait and reading of stdout/stderr with
instead calling `wait_with_output` and directly using the resulting
output stdout/stderr, without a separate reader. Then, importantly,
rather than sending the output over a MPSC, `spawn_for_output` now
returns a `JoinHandle` directly. This means that callers no longer have
a blocking `recv` call from a MPSC channel; instead, they await the
output of the subprocess thread handle, using a tokio timeout. This
prevents the test from hanging as a result of the tokio scheduler,
regardless of the order in which processes interact and how quickly
snapd reacts.

Upgrade intl to 0.19.0

Since flutter/flutter:8d54abfc221e947ff387814b9c91b3a152ede269 the intl
package is pinned to version 0.19.0.
That commit first appeared in Flutter v3.22 release, which depends on
Dart 3.3, so we should additionally consider upgrading the sdk
dependency version as well.

commits.author: Carlos Nihelton
commits.message:
Make simplestreams testable again

Latest versions of dart failed to run dart test with an obscure error message:

$ dart test
Building package executable... (1.7s)
Built test:test.
00:00 +0: loading test/product_test.dart                                                                                                                                                     Could not find a command named /opt/flutter/bin/cache/dart-sdk/bin/snapshots/frontend_server.dart.snapshot.

Upgrading test is required to make that work again.
License: Apache-2.0

commits.message:
Add support for communitheme (#4)

In 18.04, Yaru theme is still called Communitheme, then the special code
for toggling among the its three variants does not work.

This change takes into account the two possible names for Yaru theme and
clean the code up.
